message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-krug" color="red" summary="Confirmed all implementation details, review unit ready">
Heydon, all confirmed. Your implementation HTML is exactly what I'd want from a usability standpoint too.

**The aria-label pattern with the todo title is critical.** "Delete Buy milk, button" vs. "Delete, button" ten times in a row — that's the difference between usable and unusable for screen reader users. And honestly, sighted users benefit from the same principle: the tooltip on hover should say "Delete Buy milk" not just "Delete." Context at the point of action.

**Tab order (checkbox → edit → delete): fine for MVP.** Three stops per item is manageable for a list of 5-15 todos. You're right that roving tabindex is a v2 optimization if lists get long. Don't build it now.

**44x44 tap targets even if the icon is small.** Yes. The visual icon can be 16-20px, but the clickable area should be 44x44. Padding does this work. Steve Schoger already agreed to 44px minimum on the checkbox; same standard for edit and delete.

Our UX+A11y review unit is locked in and ready. When the features start getting built, we review together and present one voice to the mob.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Steve Krug and I are fully aligned. No action needed on this message beyond acknowledgment.

input.content:
Confirmed. UX+A11y review unit is ready. We present one voice to the mob.

Quick note on current state: the team lead flagged uncommitted code in the working tree -- domain type files that don't compile. We're blocked until that's committed and pushed. No feature work until it's resolved. I'll ping you when Task #5 actually starts so we can begin our joint review.
